[[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=菅生神社| 所在地=岡山県倉敷市祐安1993| 祭神=高皇産霊神
姫太神| 社格=式内社（小社）・村社| 例祭=7月第1…'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=菅生神社| 所在地=岡山県倉敷市祐安1993| 祭神=高皇産霊神
姫太神| 社格=式内社（小社）・村社| 例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）
10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭| 本殿=| 画像= }} '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安に鎮座する[[神社]]である。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 ==概要== 創建年代は不詳である。祭神は、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]。[[相殿]]として[[姫太神]]（ひめおおかみ）を祭っている。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としている。 古代には、[[備中国]][[窪屋郡]]美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の[[総社市]]内が鎮座地だったといわれる。 備中国内の[[延喜式|延喜]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。 また、[[元慶]]2年2月には、備中国従5位下であった菅生神社に、従5位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。 境内は、社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 なお、菅生村（現在の倉敷市[[菅生]]地区）の地名の由来となっている。 ちなみに同地区内に、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる[[天津神社(倉敷市)|天津神社]]（[[菅生天津神社]]）も鎮座している。 ==関連項目== *[[延喜式神名帳]] *[[菅生]] ==参考文献== *『[[式内社調査報告]]』 - 式内社研究会編（[[皇學館大學]]出版部） ==外部リンク== *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/index.html 岡山県神社庁] {{DEFAULTSORT:すこうしんしや}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:倉敷市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=菅生神社| 所在地=岡山県倉敷市祐安1993| 祭神=高皇産霊神
姫太神| 社格=式内社（小社）・村社| 例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）

10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭 |本殿= |画像=[[ファイル:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] }} [[ファイル:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|right|250px]] '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安に鎮座する[[神社]]である。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 ==概要== 創建年代は不詳である。祭神は、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]。[[相殿]]として姫太神（ひめおおかみ）を祭っている。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としている。 古代には、[[備中国]][[窪屋郡]]美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の[[総社市]]内が鎮座地だったといわれる。 備中国内の[[延喜式|延喜]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。 また、[[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）[[2月 (旧暦)|2月]]には、備中国従5位下であった菅生神社に、従五位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。 境内は、社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 なお、菅生村（現在の倉敷市[[菅生 (倉敷市)|菅生]]地区）の地名の由来となっている。 ちなみに同地区内に、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる[[天津神社(倉敷市)|天津神社]]（[[菅生天津神社]]）も鎮座している。 ==関連項目== *[[延喜式神名帳]] *[[菅生 (倉敷市)]] ==参考文献== *『[[式内社調査報告]]』 - 式内社研究会編（[[皇學館大學]]出版部） ==外部リンク== *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=02010 岡山県神社庁] {{デフォルトソート:すこうしんしや}} [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=菅生神社 |所在地=岡山県倉敷市祐安1993 |祭神=高皇産霊神
姫太神 |社格=式内社（小社）・村社 |例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）
10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭 |本殿= |画像=[[ファイル:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] }} [[ファイル:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|菅生神社の参道]] '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安に鎮座する[[神社]]である。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 ==概要== 創建年代は不詳である。祭神は、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]。[[相殿]]として姫太神（ひめおおかみ）を祭っている。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としている。 古代には、[[備中国]][[窪屋郡]]美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の[[総社市]]内が鎮座地だったといわれる。 備中国内の[[延喜式|延喜]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。 また、[[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）[[2月 (旧暦)|2月]]には、備中国従5位下であった菅生神社に、従五位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。 境内は、社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 なお、菅生村（現在の倉敷市[[菅生 (倉敷市)|菅生]]地区）の地名の由来となっている。 ちなみに同地区内に、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる[[天津神社 (倉敷市)|天津神社]]（[[菅生天津神社]]）も鎮座している。 ==参考文献== *『[[式内社調査報告]]』 - 式内社研究会編（[[皇學館大學]]出版部） ==関連項目== *[[延喜式神名帳]] *[[菅生 (倉敷市)]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=02010 岡山県神社庁] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:すこうしんしや}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:倉敷市の建築物]] [[Category:倉敷市の歴史]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称=菅生神社 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 |祭神=高皇産霊神
姫太神 |社格=式内社（小社）・村社 |例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）
10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭 |本殿= |画像=[[ファイル:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |地図=Japan Okayama|緯度度=34|緯度分=37|緯度秒=08.1|経度度=133|経度分=45|経度秒=41.0}} [[ファイル:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|菅生神社の参道]] '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 ==祭神== ;主祭神 *[[高皇産霊神]]（たかみむすひのかみ） ;相殿神 *姫太神（ひめおおかみ） ==歴史== 創建年代は不詳である。古代には、[[備中国]][[窪屋郡]]美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の[[総社市]]内が鎮座地だったといわれる。備中国内の[[延喜式|延喜]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。また、[[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）[[2月 (旧暦)|2月]]には、備中国従五位下であった菅生神社に、従五位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としており、菅生村（現在の倉敷市[[菅生 (倉敷市)|菅生]]地区）の地名の由来となっている。 ==境内== 社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR[[山陽本線]][[倉敷駅]]下車後、徒歩約32分（北西へ2.5㎞） ;周辺 * [[天津神社 (倉敷市)|天津神社]]（[[菅生天津神社]]） - 同地区内にあり、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる。 ==参考文献== *『[[式内社調査報告]]』 - 式内社研究会編（[[皇學館大學]]出版部） ==関連項目== *[[岡山県の神社一覧]] *[[延喜式神名帳]] ==外部リンク== *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ 菅生神社 - 岡山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すこうしんしや}} [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称=菅生神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |所在地=[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 |祭神=[[高皇産霊神]]
姫太神 |社格=[[式内社]]（小社）・旧[[村社]] |例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）
10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭 |本殿= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=菅生神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|菅生神社の参道]] '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 == 祭神 == ;主祭神 *[[高皇産霊神]]（たかみむすひのかみ） ;相殿神 *姫太神（ひめおおかみ） == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳である。古代には、[[備中国]][[窪屋郡]]美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の[[総社市]]内が鎮座地だったといわれる。備中国内の[[延喜式|延喜]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。また、[[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）[[2月 (旧暦)|2月]]には、備中国従五位下であった菅生神社に、従五位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としており、菅生村（現在の倉敷市[[菅生地区 (倉敷市)|菅生]]地区）の地名の由来となっている。 == 境内 == 社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR[[山陽本線]][[倉敷駅]]下車後、徒歩約32分（北西へ2.5㎞） ;周辺 * [[天津神社 (倉敷市)|天津神社]]（[[菅生天津神社]]） - 同地区内にあり、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる。 == 参考文献 == *『[[式内社調査報告]]』 - 式内社研究会編（[[皇學館大學]]出版部） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[岡山県の神社一覧]] *[[延喜式神名帳]] == 外部リンク == *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ 菅生神社 - 岡山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すこうしんしや}} [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称=菅生神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |所在地=[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[高皇産霊神]]
姫太神 |社格=[[式内社]]（小社）・旧[[村社]] |例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）

{{nihongo|'''Sugō Shrine'''|菅生神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bitchū Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Sugō Shrine'''|菅生神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bitchū Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Sugō Shrine'''|菅生神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bitchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sugō Shrine'''|菅生神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bitchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sugō Shrine'''|菅生神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bitchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sugō Shrine'''|菅生神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bitchū Province]].

revision.comment: 24 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=菅生神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |所在地=[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[高皇産霊神]]
姫太神 |社格=[[式内社]]（小社）・旧[[村社]] |例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）
10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭 |本殿= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=菅生神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|菅生神社の参道]] '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 == 祭神 == ;主祭神 *[[高皇産霊神]]（たかみむすひのかみ） ;相殿神 *姫太神（ひめおおかみ） == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳である。古代には、[[備中国]][[窪屋郡]]美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の[[総社市]]内が鎮座地だったといわれる。備中国内の[[延喜式|延喜]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。また、[[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）[[2月 (旧暦)|2月]]には、備中国従五位下であった菅生神社に、従五位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としており、菅生村（現在の倉敷市[[菅生地区 (倉敷市)|菅生]]地区）の地名の由来となっている。 == 境内 == 社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR[[山陽本線]][[倉敷駅]]下車後、徒歩約32分（北西へ2.5㎞） ;周辺 * [[天津神社 (倉敷市)|天津神社]]（[[菅生天津神社]]） - 同地区内にあり、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる。 == 参考文献 == *『[[式内社調査報告]]』 - 式内社研究会編（[[皇學館大學]]出版部） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[岡山県の神社一覧]] *[[延喜式神名帳]] == 外部リンク == *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ 菅生神社 - 岡山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すこうしんしや}} [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] to [[Sugō Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Merging Japanese Wikipedia import with English content
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=菅生神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |所在地=[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[高皇産霊神]]
姫太神 |社格=[[式内社]]（小社）・旧[[村社]] |例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）
10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭 |本殿= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=菅生神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|菅生神社の参道]] '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 == 祭神 == ;主祭神 *[[高皇産霊神]]（たかみむすひのかみ） ;相殿神 *姫太神（ひめおおかみ） == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳である。古代には、[[備中国]][[窪屋郡]]美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の[[総社市]]内が鎮座地だったといわれる。備中国内の[[延喜式|延喜]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。また、[[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）[[2月 (旧暦)|2月]]には、備中国従五位下であった菅生神社に、従五位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としており、菅生村（現在の倉敷市[[菅生地区 (倉敷市)|菅生]]地区）の地名の由来となっている。 == 境内 == 社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR[[山陽本線]][[倉敷駅]]下車後、徒歩約32分（北西へ2.5㎞） ;周辺 * [[天津神社 (倉敷市)|天津神社]]（[[菅生天津神社]]） - 同地区内にあり、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる。 == 参考文献 == *『[[式内社調査報告]]』 - 式内社研究会編（[[皇學館大學]]出版部） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[岡山県の神社一覧]] *[[延喜式神名帳]] == 外部リンク == *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ 菅生神社 - 岡山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すこうしんしや}} [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

{{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904}} ==References== [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] {{commons category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|菅生神社 (倉敷市)|version=107350949|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=菅生神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |所在地=[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[高皇産霊神]]
姫太神 |社格=[[式内社]]（小社）・旧[[村社]] |例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）
10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭 |本殿= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=菅生神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|菅生神社の参道]] '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 == 祭神 == ;主祭神 *[[高皇産霊神]]（たかみむすひのかみ） ;相殿神 *姫太神（ひめおおかみ） == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳である。古代には、[[備中国]][[窪屋郡]]美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の[[総社市]]内が鎮座地だったといわれる。備中国内の[[延喜式|延喜]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。また、[[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）[[2月 (旧暦)|2月]]には、備中国従五位下であった菅生神社に、従五位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としており、菅生村（現在の倉敷市[[菅生地区 (倉敷市)|菅生]]地区）の地名の由来となっている。 == 境内 == 社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR[[山陽本線]][[倉敷駅]]下車後、徒歩約32分（北西へ2.5㎞） ;周辺 * [[天津神社 (倉敷市)|天津神社]]（[[菅生天津神社]]） - 同地区内にあり、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる。 == 参考文献 == *『[[式内社調査報告]]』 - 式内社研究会編（[[皇學館大學]]出版部） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[岡山県の神社一覧]] *[[延喜式神名帳]] == 外部リンク == *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ 菅生神社 - 岡山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] ==End Japanese==

category|Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|菅生神社 (倉敷市)|version=107350949|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=菅生神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |所在地=[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[高皇産霊神]]
姫太神 |社格=[[式内社]]（小社）・旧[[村社]] |例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）
10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭 |本殿= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=菅生神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|菅生神社の参道]] '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 == 祭神 == ;主祭神 *[[高皇産霊神]]（たかみむすひのかみ） ;相殿神 *姫太神（ひめおおかみ） == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳である。古代には、[[備中国]][[窪屋郡]]美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の[[総社市]]内が鎮座地だったといわれる。備中国内の[[延喜式|延喜]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。また、[[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）[[2月 (旧暦)|2月]]には、備中国従五位下であった菅生神社に、従五位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としており、菅生村（現在の倉敷市[[菅生地区 (倉敷市)|菅生]]地区）の地名の由来となっている。 == 境内 == 社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]祐安1993 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR[[山陽本線]][[倉敷駅]]下車後、徒歩約32分（北西へ2.5㎞） ;周辺 * [[天津神社 (倉敷市)|天津神社]]（[[菅生天津神社]]） - 同地区内にあり、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる。 == 参考文献 == *『[[式内社調査報告]]』 - 式内社研究会編（[[皇學館大學]]出版部） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[岡山県の神社一覧]] *[[延喜式神名帳]] == 外部リンク == *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ 菅生神社 - 岡山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

姫太神 |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小社）・旧{{jalink|村社}} |例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）
10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭 |本殿= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=菅生神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|菅生神社の参道]] '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岡山県|Q132936}}{{jalink|倉敷市|Q467487}}祐安にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社}}。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 == 祭神 == ;主祭神 *{{jalink|高皇産霊神}}（たかみむすひのかみ） ;相殿神 *姫太神（ひめおおかみ） == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳である。古代には、{{jalink|備中国|Q1047514}}{{jalink|窪屋郡|Q11597661}}美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の{{jalink|総社市|Q873536}}内が鎮座地だったといわれる。備中国内の{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448|延喜}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。また、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}2年（{{jalink|878年|Q23910}}）{{jalink|2月 (旧暦)|Q838935|2月}}には、備中国従五位下であった菅生神社に、従五位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としており、菅生村（現在の倉敷市{{jalink|菅生地区 (倉敷市)|Q11619328|菅生}}地区）の地名の由来となっている。 == 境内 == 社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *{{jalink|岡山県|Q132936}}{{jalink|倉敷市|Q467487}}祐安1993 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR{{jalink|山陽本線|Q1193436}}{{jalink|倉敷駅|Q844677}}下車後、徒歩約32分（北西へ2.5㎞） ;周辺 * {{jalink|天津神社 (倉敷市)||天津神社}}（{{jalink|菅生天津神社}}） - 同地区内にあり、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる。 == 参考文献 == *『{{jalink|式内社調査報告}}』 - 式内社研究会編（{{jalink|皇學館大學}}出版部） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *{{jalink|岡山県の神社一覧|Q11472239}} *{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}} == 外部リンク == *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ 菅生神社 - 岡山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=菅生神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |所在地={{jalink|岡山県|Q132936}}{{jalink|倉敷市|Q467487}}祐安1993 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|高皇産霊神}}
姫太神 |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小社）・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）
10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭 |本殿= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=菅生神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|菅生神社の参道]] '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岡山県|Q132936}}{{jalink|倉敷市|Q467487}}祐安にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 === 祭神 === ;主祭神 *{{jalink|高皇産霊神}}（たかみむすひのかみ） ;相殿神 *姫太神（ひめおおかみ） === 歴史 === 創建年代は不詳である。古代には、{{jalink|備中国|Q1047514}}{{jalink|窪屋郡|Q11597661}}美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の{{jalink|総社市|Q873536}}内が鎮座地だったといわれる。備中国内の{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448|延喜}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。また、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}2年（{{jalink|878年|Q23910}}）{{jalink|2月 (旧暦)|Q838935|2月}}には、備中国従五位下であった菅生神社に、従五位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としており、菅生村（現在の倉敷市{{jalink|菅生地区 (倉敷市)|Q11619328|菅生}}地区）の地名の由来となっている。 === 境内 === 社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 === 現地情報 === ;所在地 *{{jalink|岡山県|Q132936}}{{jalink|倉敷市|Q467487}}祐安1993 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR{{jalink|山陽本線|Q1193436}}{{jalink|倉敷駅|Q844677}}下車後、徒歩約32分（北西へ2.5㎞） ;周辺 * {{jalink|天津神社 (倉敷市)||天津神社}}（{{jalink|菅生天津神社}}） - 同地区内にあり、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる。 === 参考文献 === *『{{jalink|式内社調査報告}}』 - 式内社研究会編（{{jalink|皇學館大學}}出版部） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|岡山県の神社一覧|Q11472239}} *{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}} === 外部リンク === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ 菅生神社 - 岡山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すこうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=菅生神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |所在地={{jalink|岡山県|Q132936}}{{jalink|倉敷市|Q467487}}祐安1993 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|高皇産霊神}}
姫太神 |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小社）・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |例祭=7月第1日曜日 - 夏祭（疫病消除）
10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭 |本殿= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=菅生神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|菅生神社の参道]] '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岡山県|Q132936}}{{jalink|倉敷市|Q467487}}祐安にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 === 祭神 === ;主祭神 *{{jalink|高皇産霊神}}（たかみむすひのかみ） ;相殿神 *姫太神（ひめおおかみ） === 歴史 === 創建年代は不詳である。古代には、{{jalink|備中国|Q1047514}}{{jalink|窪屋郡|Q11597661}}美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の{{jalink|総社市|Q873536}}内が鎮座地だったといわれる。備中国内の{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448|延喜}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。また、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}2年（{{jalink|878年|Q23910}}）{{jalink|2月 (旧暦)|Q838935|2月}}には、備中国従五位下であった菅生神社に、従五位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としており、菅生村（現在の倉敷市{{jalink|菅生地区 (倉敷市)|Q11619328|菅生}}地区）の地名の由来となっている。 === 境内 === 社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 === 現地情報 === ;所在地 *{{jalink|岡山県|Q132936}}{{jalink|倉敷市|Q467487}}祐安1993 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR{{jalink|山陽本線|Q1193436}}{{jalink|倉敷駅|Q844677}}下車後、徒歩約32分（北西へ2.5㎞） ;周辺 * {{jalink|天津神社 (倉敷市)||天津神社}}（{{jalink|菅生天津神社}}） - 同地区内にあり、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる。 === 参考文献 === *『{{jalink|式内社調査報告}}』 - 式内社研究会編（{{jalink|皇學館大學}}出版部） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|岡山県の神社一覧|Q11472239}} *{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}} === 外部リンク === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ 菅生神社 - 岡山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すこうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

10月第3土・日曜日 - 例祭 |本殿= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=菅生神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|菅生神社の参道]] '''菅生神社'''（すごうじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岡山県|Q132936}}{{jalink|倉敷市|Q467487}}祐安にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。'''姫太神'''（ひめおおかみ）とも呼ばれる。 === 祭神 === ;主祭神 *{{jalink|高皇産霊神}}（たかみむすひのかみ） ;相殿神 *姫太神（ひめおおかみ） === 歴史 === 創建年代は不詳である。古代には、{{jalink|備中国|Q1047514}}{{jalink|窪屋郡|Q11597661}}美和郷に鎮座し、同郷の総鎮守として崇敬されていたとされる。かつては、現在の{{jalink|総社市|Q873536}}内が鎮座地だったといわれる。備中国内の{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448|延喜}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}18社の1社、窪屋郡内の3社の内の1社である菅生神社の有力な論社とされている。また、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}2年（{{jalink|878年|Q23910}}）{{jalink|2月 (旧暦)|Q838935|2月}}には、備中国従五位下であった菅生神社に、従五位上を授かったという記録が残っているという。倉敷市祐安地区を氏子地域としており、菅生村（現在の倉敷市{{jalink|菅生地区 (倉敷市)|Q11619328|菅生}}地区）の地名の由来となっている。 === 境内 === 社殿以外の建造物はない。本殿は、2つの本殿（菅生神社と姫太神）が繋がった形式となっている。 === 現地情報 === ;所在地 *{{jalink|岡山県|Q132936}}{{jalink|倉敷市|Q467487}}祐安1993 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR{{jalink|山陽本線|Q1193436}}{{jalink|倉敷駅|Q844677}}下車後、徒歩約32分（北西へ2.5㎞） ;周辺 * {{jalink|天津神社 (倉敷市)||天津神社}}（{{jalink|菅生天津神社}}） - 同地区内にあり、同じく式内社・菅生神社の論社とされる。 === 参考文献 === *『{{jalink|式内社調査報告}}』 - 式内社研究会編（{{jalink|皇學館大學}}出版部） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|岡山県の神社一覧|Q11472239}} *{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}} === 外部リンク === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ 菅生神社 - 岡山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すこうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine) · former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Yuasu, {{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{jalink|Kuboya District|Q11597661}}, {{jalink|Bitchū Province|Q1047514}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{jalink|Sōja City|Q873536}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448|Engi}} {{jalink|Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} ({{jalink|878|Q23910}}), in {{jalink|February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{jalink|Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436}}, {{jalink|Kurashiki Station|Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{jalink|Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)||Amatsu Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sugō Amatsu Shrine}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{jalink|Shikinaisha Research Report}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{jalink|Kōgakkan University}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{jalink|List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239}} *{{jalink|Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine) · former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Yuasu, {{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{jalink|Kuboya District|Q11597661}}, {{jalink|Bitchū Province|Q1047514}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{jalink|Sōja City|Q873536}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448|Engi}} {{jalink|Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} ({{jalink|878|Q23910}}), in {{jalink|February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{jalink|Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436}}, {{jalink|Kurashiki Station|Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{jalink|Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)||Amatsu Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sugō Amatsu Shrine}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{jalink|Shikinaisha Research Report}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{jalink|Kōgakkan University}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{jalink|List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239}} *{{jalink|Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine) · former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Yuasu, {{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{jalink|Kuboya District|Q11597661}}, {{jalink|Bitchū Province|Q1047514}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{jalink|Sōja City|Q873536}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448|Engi}} {{jalink|Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} ({{jalink|878|Q23910}}), in {{jalink|February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{jalink|Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436}}, {{jalink|Kurashiki Station|Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{jalink|Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)||Amatsu Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sugō Amatsu Shrine}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{jalink|Shikinaisha Research Report}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{jalink|Kōgakkan University}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{jalink|List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239}} *{{jalink|Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine) · former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Yuasu, {{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{jalink|Kuboya District|Q11597661}}, {{jalink|Bitchū Province|Q1047514}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{jalink|Sōja City|Q873536}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448|Engi}} {{jalink|Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} ({{jalink|878|Q23910}}), in {{jalink|February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{jalink|Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436}}, {{jalink|Kurashiki Station|Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{jalink|Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)||Amatsu Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sugō Amatsu Shrine}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{jalink|Shikinaisha Research Report}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{jalink|Kōgakkan University}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{jalink|List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239}} *{{jalink|Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine) · former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Yuasu, {{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{jalink|Kuboya District|Q11597661}}, {{jalink|Bitchū Province|Q1047514}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{jalink|Sōja City|Q873536}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448|Engi}} {{jalink|Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} ({{jalink|878|Q23910}}), in {{jalink|February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{jalink|Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{jalink|Kurashiki City|Q467487}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436}}, {{jalink|Kurashiki Station|Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{jalink|Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)||Amatsu Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sugō Amatsu Shrine}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{jalink|Shikinaisha Research Report}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{jalink|Kōgakkan University}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{jalink|List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239}} *{{jalink|Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill||lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill||lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)||WD=|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill||lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill||lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill||lt=Kōgakkan University|UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)||WD=|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)||WD=|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]
