'ja'}https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ {{commons category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661|1=Kuboya district}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910|1=878}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February|1=February (Lunisolar)}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō|1=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677|1=Kurashiki Station}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239|1=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

'ja'}https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ {{commons category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661|1=Kuboya district}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910|1=878}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February|1=February (Lunisolar)}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō|1=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677|1=Kurashiki Station}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239|1=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugō Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

'ja'}https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ {{commons category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661|1=Kuboya district}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910|1=878}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February|1=February (Lunisolar)}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō|1=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677|1=Kurashiki Station}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239|1=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

'ja'}https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ {{commons category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661|1=Kuboya district}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910|1=878}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February|1=February (Lunisolar)}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō|1=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677|1=Kurashiki Station}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239|1=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]]

'ja'}https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ {{commons category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661|1=Kuboya district}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910|1=878}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February|1=February (Lunisolar)}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō|1=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677|1=Kurashiki Station}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239|1=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Okayama Prefecture]]

'ja'}https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ {{commons category|Category:Sugo-jinja (Kurashiki)}} [[JA:菅生神社 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619345}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Sugō Shrine |Image=[[File:Sugou-jinja, Kurashiki.JPG|250px]] |Location={{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661|1=Kuboya district}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910|1=878}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February|1=February (Lunisolar)}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō|1=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677|1=Kurashiki Station}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239|1=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Okayama Prefecture]]

City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}} Yuasu 1993 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
Himeōkami |Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor shrine) · former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Annual festivals=First Sunday of July – Summer Festival (warding off epidemics)
Third Saturday and Sunday of October – Annual Festival |Main hall= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Sugō Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sugou Shinto shrine approach to a shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Approach to Sugō Shrine]] '''Sugō Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yuasu, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also called '''Himeōkami''' (姫太神). === Enshrined deities === ;Primary deity *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすひのかみ) ;Associated deity *Himeōkami (ひめおおかみ) === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In ancient times, it is said to have been enshrined in Miwa-go, {{ill|lt=Kuboya District|Q11597661|WD=Q11597661|1=Kuboya district}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}}, where it was revered as the chief guardian shrine of the area. It is also said that its former location was within what is now {{ill|lt=Sōja City|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}. Sugō Shrine is considered one of the leading candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Sugō Shrine listed as one of the 18 shrines in Bitchū Province recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engi|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and one of the three within Kuboya District. There is also a record stating that in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910|1=878}}), in {{ill|lt=February (lunar calendar)|Q838935|WD=Q838935|lt=February|1=February (Lunisolar)}}, the shrine—then ranked Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in Bitchū Province—was granted promotion to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. The parish area (ujiko) covers the Yuasu district of Kurashiki City, and the shrine is the origin of the place name Sugō Village (now the {{ill|lt=Sugō district (Kurashiki City)|Q11619328|WD=Q11619328|lt=Sugō|1=Sugō}} district of Kurashiki City). === Shrine grounds === There are no buildings other than the shrine structures. The main sanctuary consists of two main halls (Sugō Shrine and Himeōkami) connected together. === Visitor information === ;Address *Yuasu 1993, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki City|Q467487|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}} ;Access *Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Kurashiki Station|Q844677|WD=Q844677|1=Kurashiki Station}}; approximately 32 minutes on foot (2.5 km to the northwest) ;Nearby * {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Kurashiki City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugō Amatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Located in the same district; likewise a Shikinaisha and considered a candidate shrine for Sugō Shrine. === References === *''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Research Report|1=UNKNOWN}}'' – Edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === *{{ill|lt=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture|Q11472239|WD=Q11472239|1=List of shrines in Okayama Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Register of Shinto Deities|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} === External links === *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16645/ Sugō Shrine – Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugō Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kurashiki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Okayama Prefecture]]
